volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Merchant (Section 3.1.542 - Section 3.4.619)

sections.label: Section 3.1.542
sections.translation: What ? His teeth ?

sections.label: Section 3.1.542b
sections.original: Nihil est . sequere sis . hunc me diem unum oravit
sections.translation: It ’ s no matter ? Follow me this way , please ; he requested that I would find you room for one day in my house , since my wife is away in the country .

sections.label: Section 3.2.544
sections.original: Tandem impetravi ut egomet me corrumperem :
sections.translation: At last I ’ ve managed to ruin myself ;

sections.label: Section 3.2.545
sections.original: emptast amica clam uxorem et clam filium .
sections.translation: a mistress has been purchased for me without the knowledge of my wife and son . I ’ m resolved on it ; I ’ ll have recourse again to former habits and enjoy myself . In my allotment of existence , almost now run through , the little that there remains of life , I ’ ll cheer up with pleasure , wine , and love . For it ’ s quite proper for this time of life to enjoy itself .

sections.label: Section 3.2.550
sections.original: adulescens quom sis , tum quom est sanguis integer ,
sections.translation: When you are young , then , when the blood is fresh , it ’ s right to devote your exertions to acquiring your fortune ; and then when at last , you are an old man , you may set yourself at your ease ; drink , and be amorous ; this , the fact that you are living , is now so much profit . This , as I say , I ’ ll carry out in deed .

sections.label: Section 3.2.555
sections.original: interea tamen huc intro ad me invisam domum :
sections.translation: Meanwhile , however , I ’ ll take a look in-doors here at my house ; my wife has been some time expecting me at home quite hungry : now , she ’ ll be worrying me to death with her scolding , if I go in-doors . But , in fine , whatever comes of it , i ’ faith , I ’ ll not go , but I ’ ll first meet this neighbour of mine before I return home ;

sections.label: Section 3.2.560
sections.original: redeam ; ut mihi aedis aliquas conducat volo ,
sections.translation: I want him to hire some house for me , where this damsel may dwell . And , see , he ’ s coming out of doors .

sections.label: Section 3.3.562
sections.original: Adducam ego illum iam ad te , si convenero .
sections.translation: I ’ ll bring him to you directly , if I meet him .

sections.label: Section 3.3.563
sections.translation: He ’ s meaning me .

sections.label: Section 3.3.563b
sections.translation: How say you , Demipho ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.563c
sections.translation: Is the damsel at your house ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.564
sections.translation: What do you suppose ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.564b
sections.translation: What if I go see her ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.564c
sections.translation: Why making such haste ? Stay .

sections.label: Section 3.3.565
sections.translation: What am I to do ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.565b
sections.original: Quod opust facto facito ut cogites .
sections.translation: What you ought to do ; take care and consider .

sections.label: Section 3.3.566
sections.original: Quid cogitem ? equidem hercle opus hoc facto existimo ,
sections.translation: Consider what ? Why troth , for my own part , I think there ’ s need for my doing this , going in-doors there , I mean .

sections.label: Section 3.3.567b
sections.translation: What , is it so , you old wether ? Would you be going in ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.568
sections.translation: What should I do else ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.568b
sections.translation: First listen to this , and attend ; there ’ s something even before this that I think it proper you should do .

sections.label: Section 3.3.570
sections.original: nam nunc si illuc intro ieris , amplecti voles ,
sections.translation: For if you now go in-doors to her , you ’ ll be wishing to embrace her , chatting with her , and kissing her .

sections.label: Section 3.3.571b
sections.translation: Really you know my feelings ; you understand what I would be at .

sections.label: Section 3.3.573
sections.translation: You will be doing wrong .

sections.label: Section 3.3.573b
sections.translation: What , with that which you love ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.573c
sections.translation: So much the less reason . Would you , full of hungriness , with a foul breath ,

sections.label: Section 3.3.575
sections.translation: a stinking old fellow , be kissing a woman ? And wouldn ’ t you , as you approached , be setting a female vomiting ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.578
sections.original: Quid si igitur unum faciam hoc ? si censes , coquom
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I ’ m sure that you ’ re in love , as you point out these things beforehand to me . What then , if I give a dinner ? If you approve of this , let ’ s lay hold of some cook , who may be cooking away a meal [*]

sections.label: Section 3.3.580
sections.original: apud te hic usque ad vesperum .
sections.translation: here at your house , even until the evening .

sections.label: Section 3.3.580b
sections.translation: Well , I ’ m of that way of thinking . Now you are talking wisely , and like a lover .

sections.label: Section 3.3.582
sections.original: Quid stamus ? quin ergo imus atque obsonium
sections.translation: Why are we standing here ? Why then don ’ t we be off and procure the provisions , that we may be comfortable ?

sections.label: Section 3.3.583b
sections.translation: For my part , I ’ ll follow you . And , i ’ troth , you ’ ll be finding out a lodging for her , if you are prudent ;

sections.label: Section 3.3.585
sections.original: nullum hercle praeter hunc diem illa apud med erit .
sections.translation: for , i ’ faith , she shan ’ t be at my house a single day beyond the present ; I ’ m afraid of my wife , lest , if she should return from the country to-morrow , she ’ ll be finding her here .

sections.label: Section 3.4.588
sections.original: Súmne ego homo miser , qui nusquam bene queo quiescere ?
sections.translation: Am I not a wretched mortal , who can rest quietly nowhere ? If I ’ m at home , my mind ’ s abroad ; but if I ’ m abroad , my mind ’ s at home .

sections.label: Section 3.4.590
sections.original: ita mi in pectore atque in corde facit amor incendium :
sections.translation: To such a degree . has love kindled a flame in my breast and in my heart ; did not the tears fall from my eyes , why then , I doubt , my head would be on fire . I cling to hope ; safety I ’ ve lost ; whether she ’ ll return or no , I know not . If my father seizes her , as he has said , then my welfare is gone in exile ; but if my companion has done what he promised , then my welfare has not departed .

sections.label: Section 3.4.595
sections.original: sed tamen dem si podagrosis pedibus esset Eutychus ,
sections.translation: But still , even if Eutychus had had gouty feet , he could have been back from the harbour by this . This is a very great fault of his , that he is too slow , against the wishes of my feelings . But ( looking towards the side ) isn ’ t this he whom I espy running ? ’ Tis he himself ; I ’ ll go meet him . ( Clasps his hands .) Thou who art the overlooker of Gods and of men and the mistress of mortals as well , inasmuch as thou hast indulged me in this hope that I entertained , I do return thee thanks . Does any hope remain ? Alas ! I ’ m utterly undone . His countenance by no means pleases me !

sections.label: Section 3.4.600
sections.original: tristis incédit ( pectus ardet , haereo ), quassat caput .
sections.translation: He moves along in sadness . My breast burns . I am in doubt . He shakes his head . Eutychus !

sections.label: Section 3.4.601b
sections.translation: Alas ! Charinus .

sections.label: Section 3.4.601c
sections.translation: Before you take breath , in one word , speak out . Where am I ? Here , or among the dead ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.603
sections.original: Neque apud mortuos neque hic es .
sections.translation: You are neither among the dead nor here .

sections.label: Section 3.4.603b
sections.translation: I ’ m saved , immortality has been vouchsafed me — he has purchased her . He has nicely tricked my father .

sections.label: Section 3.4.605
sections.original: impetrabilior qui vivat nullus est . dice , obsecro :
sections.translation: There ’ s no one living more clever at gaining his purpose . Prithee , do tell me ; if I ’ m neither here nor at Acheron , where am I ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.606b
sections.translation: Nowhere in the world .

sections.label: Section 3.4.607
sections.original: Disperii , illaec interemit me modó oratio .
sections.translation: I ’ m utterly undone ! That speech has just put an end to me here .

sections.label: Section 3.4.608
sections.original: Odiosast oratio , cum rém agas longinquom loqui .
sections.translation: It is an annoying way of speaking , when you should despatch the business , to be beating about the bush [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.4.609
sections.original: Quidquid est , ad capita rerum perveni .
sections.translation: Whatever it is , do come to the material points of the matter .

sections.label: Section 3.4.609b
sections.translation: First of all ,

sections.label: Section 3.4.610
sections.translation: we are ruined .

sections.label: Section 3.4.610b
sections.original: Quin tu illud potius nuntias quod nescio ?
sections.translation: But why don ’ t you in preference tell me that which I don ’ t know ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.611
sections.translation: The damsel has been taken away from you .

sections.label: Section 3.4.611b
sections.translation: Eutychus , you are guilty of a capital offence .

sections.label: Section 3.4.612
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.612b
sections.original: Quia aequalem et sodalem , liberum civem , enicas .
sections.translation: Because you are killing your year ’ s-mate and friend , a free citizen .

sections.label: Section 3.4.613
sections.translation: May the Gods forbid it !

sections.label: Section 3.4.613b
sections.original: Demisisti gladium in iugulum : iam cadam .
sections.translation: You ’ ve thrust a sword into my throat ; this moment I shall fall .

sections.label: Section 3.4.614
sections.translation: Troth now , prithee , don ’ t be desponding in mind .

sections.label: Section 3.4.614b
sections.translation: I have none to be desponding in .

sections.label: Section 3.4.615
sections.original: loquere porro aliam malam rem . cui est empta ?
sections.translation: Tell on , then , the rest of your bad news ; for whom has she been purchased ?

sections.label: Section 3.4.615b
sections.translation: I don ’ t know . She had been already knocked down to the bidder and taken off by him , when I got to the harbour .

sections.label: Section 3.4.616b
sections.translation: Ah me ! Already , indeed , have you heaped burning mountains of woe upon me . Proceed , executioner , torment me on , since you have once begun .

sections.label: Section 3.4.619
sections.original: Non tibi istuc mágis dividiaest , quam mihí hodie fuit .
sections.translation: This is not more a cause of anguish to yourself , than it has proved to me this day .
